text.title: ELK Stack Architecture and Deployment Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules grounded in Elastic best-practice + production incident postmortems.

rule.statement: Cluster MUST split master, data, and coordinating roles into separate nodes. Mixed-role nodes are forbidden in prod.
rule.rationale: Mixed roles cause master instability under data-node pressure. Separation isolates failure modes + lets each role be sized correctly.

rule.statement: Prod cluster MUST use hot-warm-cold tier architecture for time-series logs. Single-tier setups are forbidden for retention > 30 days.
rule.rationale: Hot tier (SSD/NVMe) holds recent + searched-often data; warm (HDD/EBS gp3) holds aged data; cold (searchable snapshot to S3) holds rarely-accessed. Reduces storage cost 70-90%.

rule.statement: Cluster MUST have exactly 3 master-eligible nodes (or 5+ for very large clusters). 1 and 2 master setups are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Single master = no HA. Two masters = split-brain risk. Three masters tolerate one failure with quorum.

rule.statement: Spec MUST include a numeric capacity plan: GB/day in × retention × replication = total storage. Heap MUST be ≤ 31 GB per JVM.
rule.rationale: Undocumented capacity plans become 'cluster red' incidents at 2 a.m. The 31 GB heap limit avoids loss of compressed oops.

rule.statement: Prod deployment MUST use ECK (Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes) for K8s clusters, or vendor-managed (Elastic Cloud / OpenSearch Service). Hand-rolled Docker Compose is forbidden for prod.
rule.rationale: ECK automates upgrades, master quorum, rolling restarts. Hand-rolled Compose breaks on the first upgrade.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
